title: Louisiana Purchase Cornell Notes
gradingNotes:
Expected answers:
Q1: Napoleon needed money for European wars and lost Haiti after Toussaint L'Ouverture's revolt
Q2: Doubled the size of the US, about 828,000 square miles
Q3: Lewis and Clark with Sacagawea in 1804
Q4: No constitutional authority to buy land, strict vs loose interpretation

Vocabulary should include all 5 terms. Accept student-friendly definitions.
Summary should mention: $15 million price, doubled size, Napoleon's reasons, constitutional concerns.

importedDoc.text:
Louisiana Purchase Cornell Notes

QUESTIONS:
1) Why did Napoleon sell the Louisiana Territory?
2) How did the purchase affect US size?
3) Who explored the new territory?
4) What constitutional concerns existed?
